mnemonics: LIMITS: Look In Metric-space, Isolated Means Totally Separate. A limit point is Like Infinitely Many Tiny Spheres touching it (no matter how small the sphere, you find another point).
keyPoints: A point x is a limit point of a set A if every open ball centered at x contains a point of A different from x itself.
A point a is an isolated point of A if there exists an open ball centered at a that contains no other points of A.
The set of all limit points of a set A is called the derived set, denoted A'.
commonMistakes: Confusing a limit point with a point that is simply 'close' to the set. A limit point must have other points of A infinitely close to it.
Assuming that because a point is not in the set, it cannot be a limit point. A limit point does NOT have to be an element of the set itself.
Thinking isolated points and limit points are opposites. A set can have points that are neither; a point in a set is either a limit point OR an isolated point.
